attack_surface.data_description: Frontend display data and user interaction prompts/feedback

security_gap.gap_description: Compromised frontend can alter displayed information or transaction details before signing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with frontend, trusting its integrity.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with the frontend can lead to phishing private keys or unauthorized transactions.

assumptions: Attacker can compromise the delivery mechanism of the BeraHub frontend.
User interacts with the compromised frontend.

mitigation.strategy: Implement Content Security Policy and Subresource Integrity, secure build and deployment pipeline.

description: An attacker tampers with the BeraHub frontend to steal user's private key or trick the user into signing malicious transactions.

attack_surface.data_description: Frontend display data and user interaction prompts/feedback

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend inadvertently displays or logs sensitive user interaction data.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with frontend, potentially exposing sensitive data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Sensitive data exposure can lead to privacy violations or targeted attacks.

assumptions: BeraHub frontend or associated logging reveals sensitive user data.
Attacker can access these logs or intercept frontend communication.

mitigation.strategy: Minimize data displayed and logged on frontend, encrypt sensitive communication.

description: BeraHub frontend or associated services disclose sensitive user interaction data through logs or unsecured channels.

attack_surface.data_description: User input for wallet connection

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend infrastructure is unable to handle high volume malicious requests.

actor_risk.description: User sends requests to the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Standard web infrastructure mitigates this common threat.

assumptions: BeraHub frontend lacks sufficient DoS protection mechanisms.

mitigation.strategy: Implement DDoS protection, rate limiting, and proper traffic filtering.

description: An attacker floods the BeraHub frontend with excessive requests, causing it to become unavailable to legitimate users.

attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request parameters (e.g., dApp origin, requested chain)

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious frontend spoofs legitimate wallet connection requests to phish user credentials.

actor_risk.description: User initiates wallet connection, trusting the dApp interface.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing leads to compromise of user's private key.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious BeraHub frontend.
User's wallet software does not sufficiently warn about phishing attempts.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users on wallet security, rely on wallet software warnings, use browser extensions for dApp verification.

description: A malicious BeraHub frontend spoofs the wallet connection interface to trick the user into revealing their private key or seed phrase.

attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request parameters (e.g., dApp origin, requested chain)

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious frontend tampers with connection parameters or requested permissions without user's full awareness.

actor_risk.description: User initiates wallet connection and approves requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering can lead to granting excessive permissions or connecting to an unintended network.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious BeraHub frontend.
User approves modified connection requests without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet software should clearly display requested permissions and connection details for user review.

description: A malicious BeraHub frontend tampers with the wallet connection request to gain broader permissions or connect to a different chain than intended.

attack_surface.data_description: User wallet address and public key

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend or backend stores or processes excessive user wallet data upon connection.

actor_risk.description: User connects wallet, potentially exposing wallet details.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Excessive data collection poses a privacy risk to the user.

assumptions: BeraHub backend collects and stores more user wallet data than necessary.
Backend is vulnerable to data breaches.

mitigation.strategy: Implement data minimization policies, secure data storage, and access control on backend systems.

description: BeraHub frontend or backend collects and potentially leaks sensitive user wallet data (e.g., transaction history, balances) beyond the wallet address upon connection.

attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request parameters (e.g., dApp origin, requested chain)

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious dApp requests overly broad or unnecessary permissions from the user's wallet.

actor_risk.description: User approves requested wallet permissions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Granting excessive permissions can lead to unauthorized control over user assets.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious BeraHub frontend.
User does not understand the implications of the requested permissions and approves them.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet software should present clear, granular permission requests and warnings for risky permissions.

description: A malicious dApp requests excessive permissions (e.g., unlimited token spending) from the user's wallet during the connection process.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for staking (e.g., token amount, target vault/pool)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract allows unauthorized modification of staked balances.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the staking contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering leads to loss or manipulation of staked user assets.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, access control) allowing state manipulation.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices, implement reentrancy guards.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to tamper with other users' staked PoL assets.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for staking (e.g., token amount, target vault/pool)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate staking or unstaking.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from depositing or withdrawing funds.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement robust error handling.

description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Reward Vault Contract, preventing legitimate users from staking or unstaking assets.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for staking (e.g., token amount, target vault/pool)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized administrative actions or control over staked assets.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation could lead to loss of all staked assets in the vault.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has access control vulnerabilities.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control mechanisms, follow principle of least privilege, conduct access control audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to gain unauthorized administrative control or control over other users' staked assets.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract allows claiming BGT rewards that a user is not entitled to.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the claim contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing allows theft of user assets (BGT).

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability in its reward calculation or claim entitlement logic.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust reward tracking and claim logic, conduct thorough smart contract audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to claim BGT rewards that belong to other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract allows unauthorized modification of user BGT reward balances.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the claim contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering leads to loss or manipulation of earned BGT rewards.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability allowing state manipulation of reward balances.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices for state management.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to tamper with other users' accumulated BGT reward balances.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate BGT claims or gets stuck in a state preventing claims.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from accessing earned BGT rewards.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience or has a state-locking bug.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack or exploit a bug.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement secure state management, consider emergency pause.

description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Reward Vault Contract or exploits a bug to prevent legitimate users from claiming BGT rewards.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for claiming BGT (e.g., amount, user identifier)

security_gap.gap_description: Reward Vault contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized control over claiming BGT rewards.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation could lead to unauthorized distribution or theft of BGT rewards.

assumptions: Reward Vault Contract has access control vulnerabilities related to claim functions.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control for sensitive functions, follow principle of least privilege, conduct audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Reward Vault Contract to gain unauthorized control over claiming functions (e.g., claiming for others).

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract allows unauthorized users to spoof boost contributions or amounts.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the boosting contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing undermines the Proof-of-Liquidity boosting mechanism and fairness.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract has a vulnerability in its boost tracking or verification logic.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust boost tracking and verification logic, conduct thorough smart contract audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Validator Staking Contract to spoof BGT boost contributions, unfairly influencing validator rewards.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract allows unauthorized modification of user boost amounts or total boost metrics.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the boosting contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering undermines the core PoL mechanism by manipulating boost influence.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract has a vulnerability allowing state manipulation of boost balances or system metrics.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices for state management and calculations.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Validator Staking Contract to tamper with other users' BGT boost amounts or the total boost metrics.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate boosting or unboosting.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from participating in the boosting mechanism, impacting PoL.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience or has a state-locking bug.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack or exploit a bug.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement secure state management, consider emergency pause.

description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Validator Staking Contract or exploits a bug to prevent legitimate users from boosting or unboosting BGT.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for boosting (e.g., BGT amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Validator Staking contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized control over boosting-related functions or metrics.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation could allow manipulation of the boosting mechanism, impacting validator rewards and PoL alignment.

assumptions: Validator Staking Contract has access control vulnerabilities related to boost functions.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control for sensitive functions, follow principle of least privilege, conduct audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Validator Staking Contract to gain unauthorized control over boosting functions or metrics.

attack_surface.data_description: Cryptographic proof data validating user's incentive entitlement

security_gap.gap_description: BGT Incentive Distributor contract or Backend API does not sufficiently validate incentive claim proofs, allowing spoofing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the incentive claim mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing allows theft of user assets (Incentive Tokens).

assumptions: Backend API's proof generation is flawed or BGT Incentive Distributor contract's proof verification is vulnerable.
Attacker can craft valid-looking fraudulent proofs.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a robust, audited cryptographic proof system, ensure strict on-chain verification.

description: An attacker spoofs incentive entitlement proof data from the Backend API to the BGT Incentive Distributor contract, claiming incentives they did not earn.

attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain data related to incentive entitlements and claim history

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API storing incentive entitlement data is vulnerable to unauthorized tampering.

actor_risk.description: User data is stored and processed by the backend API.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with entitlement data directly impacts user assets (Incentive Tokens).

assumptions: Backend API has vulnerabilities (e.g., injection, weak access control) allowing data modification.
Attacker can access and exploit these vulnerabilities.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure API development practices, robust access control, input validation, regular security audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Backend API to tamper with user incentive entitlement data, altering their claimable amount.

attack_surface.data_description: Request for off-chain incentive claim proof (e.g., user address, claim period)

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing proof generation or claiming.

actor_risk.description: User requests proof or claims incentives.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents users from accessing earned Incentive Tokens.

assumptions: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience.
Attacker has resources to conduct a DoS attack.

mitigation.strategy: Implement DoS protection, rate limiting for API endpoints, ensure contract resilience.

description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract, preventing users from claiming their validator incentives.

attack_surface.data_description: Cryptographic proof data validating user's incentive entitlement

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API or proof mechanism leaks sensitive data about user entitlements or claim history.

actor_risk.description: User's incentive entitlement data is processed by the backend API.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exposure of claim history or entitlement details can be a privacy violation.

assumptions: Backend API response includes excessive or sensitive data.
Attacker can intercept API communication or access API logs.

mitigation.strategy: Minimize data in API responses, implement access control to API, encrypt sensitive data transmission.

description: The Backend API or the proof generation process discloses sensitive user data related to validator incentives or claim history.

attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain data related to incentive entitlements and claim history

security_gap.gap_description: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized manipulation of incentive entitlements.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the incentive claim mechanism, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation allows manipulation of all user incentive entitlements.

assumptions: Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract has access control vulnerabilities.
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control for sensitive API endpoints and contract functions, conduct audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Backend API or BGT Incentive Distributor contract to gain unauthorized control over incentive entitlement data for any user.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract allows unauthorized users to spoof voting power or propose malicious actions.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing undermines decentralized governance and decision making.

assumptions: Governance Contract has a vulnerability in its voting power calculation, proposal submission, or verification logic.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust voting power calculation and verification, secure proposal submission checks, conduct thorough smart contract audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Governance Contract to spoof voting power or submit fraudulent governance proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract allows unauthorized modification of vote counts, proposal details, or governance parameters.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering undermines democratic governance processes and outcomes.

assumptions: Governance Contract has a vulnerability allowing state manipulation of votes, proposals, or parameters.
Attacker can craft transactions to exploit the vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits, use secure coding practices for state management, implement multi-signature for critical actions.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Governance Contract to tamper with vote counts, proposal details, or governance parameters.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract is susceptible to DoS attacks preventing legitimate voting, proposing, or proposal execution.

actor_risk.description: User sends transactions to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Crippling governance prevents necessary protocol updates and parameter changes.

assumptions: Governance Contract lacks sufficient DoS resilience or has a state-locking bug in its lifecycle management.
Attacker has resources to conduct a griefing attack or exploit a bug.

mitigation.strategy: Design contract functions to be resilient against griefing, implement secure state management for governance lifecycle, utilize Guardian oversight.

description: An attacker performs a denial of service attack on the Governance Contract or exploits a bug to prevent legitimate users from participating in governance processes.

attack_surface.data_description: Parameters for governance action (e.g., proposal details, vote choice, voting power proof)

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract has access control flaws allowing unauthorized control over critical governance functions or parameters.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the contract, potentially discovering vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation allows an attacker to take control of the entire governance process.

assumptions: Governance Contract has access control vulnerabilities related to sensitive functions (e.g., proposal execution, parameter changes).
Attacker can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated permissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust access control, require multi-signature for critical actions (Guardian system), implement time-locks for proposal execution, conduct audits.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the Governance Contract to gain unauthorized administrative control over governance processes or parameters.
